rulings.Case Title: CORNELIUS MURPHY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, MARCH 22, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 1. Defendant Exxon Mobil Corp.¿s demurrer to plaintiff¿s second amended complaint is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: W. STANLEY BURROUGHS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Stay/Dismiss Based On Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, MARCH 22, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 2. Defendant Industrial Gloves Co.¿s motion to dismiss and alternative motion to stay based on forum non conveniens are denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that Florida is a suitable alternative forum because it did not show that all remaining defendants are subject to jurisdiction there. Defendant also failed to show that California is a seriously inconvenient forum considering the balance of public and private interests. The only alleged exposure discussed by the parties occurred in California. Defendant did not show that plaintiff is even alleging exposure in Florida. Further, at this late stage of the case, the balance of the relevant considerations ¿ including all the work that has been done by the parties in reliance on this case being tried in San Francisco and avoidance of additional costs and delay associated with the filing and prosecution of a new case in Florida ¿ weigh strongly in favor of retaining the case in this forum. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HOWARD REENTS et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, MARCH 22, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 4. Defendant Schneider Electric USA¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1-4 (negligence, strict liability, survival and loss of consortium and granted as to issue 5 (punitive damages). Defendant did not sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Howard Reents was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. The deposition testimony of Howard Reents, liberally construed, precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. The affirmative evidence submitted by defendant, strictly construed, does not show that plaintiffs are unable to establish that Howard Reents was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. The declaration of Mr. Templin creates a triable issue whether Howard Reents¿ exposure to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Reents¿ asbestos-related illness. As to punitive damages, defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present clear and convincing evidence creating a triable issue that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY WAYNE KING VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, MARCH 22, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 3. Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.¿s demurrer to the first, second, third, fourth, fourteenth and seventeenth causes of action in plaintiff¿s complaint is sustained without leave to amend. The complaint fails to allege facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action against defendant, and amending the complaint cannot cure this deficiency. = (220/HEK)
